name: Feature Behaviors
related_stage_3_constraints: Every write API emits an AuditEvent.
Private event APIs require event participant authorization.
Contribution APIs must use external-reference terminology and cannot expose Crewly payout controls.
Receipt correction APIs must preserve prior extracted and corrected values.

ux_decisions.decision: Specify launch-critical feature behavior by workflow group rather than isolated controls.
ux_decisions.rationale: Feature behavior must support complete journeys and Stage 5 implementation planning.

behavior_groups.name: Trip Setup and Access
validation_rules: trip name and basic dates/location context are required before publishing
public page requires explicit publish action
behavior_groups.permission_behavior: Only organizer can manage visibility and approvals.
behavior_groups.error_behavior: Show inline validation and preserve draft state.

behavior_groups.name: Public Funding and Updates
validation_rules: external contribution target must be a valid provider URL/reference
public updates require explicit audience selection
public photo highlights require organizer confirmation
behavior_groups.permission_behavior: Public viewers can read public content; organizer controls provider target and public promotion.
behavior_groups.error_behavior: If provider target is unavailable, show contribution unavailable state without blocking public page.

behavior_groups.name: Trip Ledger and Receipts
system_responses: show translated/display values as derived data
validation_rules: expense amount, currency, paid-by, and scope are required
receipt corrections must retain original extraction history
display FX cannot overwrite source amount
behavior_groups.permission_behavior: Approved trip members can create trip expenses and receipts; event receipts require event participant access.
behavior_groups.error_behavior: OCR/translation/FX failure falls back to manual entry and original values.

behavior_groups.name: Private Events
system_responses: filter participant list to approved trip members
validation_rules: participants must be approved trip members
event expenses must remain event scoped
payment status cannot trigger in-app settlement
behavior_groups.permission_behavior: Only event participants and admins can view event details.
behavior_groups.error_behavior: Permission-denied state explains event access boundary without revealing private event data.

behavior_groups.name: Media, Notifications, Moderation, and Audit
validation_rules: public promotion requires explicit organizer action
reports require target content or context
behavior_groups.permission_behavior: Admins can review reports and audit events; ordinary users cannot access admin records.
behavior_groups.error_behavior: Failed media upload or notification delivery shows retry/pending state.

global_behavior_rules: Every sensitive write creates visible success/failure feedback and an audit event.
Any public exposure action requires explicit audience selection or confirmation.
Any async process exposes pending, success, failed, retry, and manual fallback states.
External contribution behavior must never imply Crewly has custody of funds.

risks_and_constraints.risk: Public contribution setup could look like a native payout account flow.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Use external provider target language and omit Crewly payout/account balance controls.

risks_and_constraints.risk: Receipt localization could be mistaken for authoritative financial truth.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Label original, extracted, translated, and display currency values distinctly.

stage_5_handoff_notes: Each behavior group should become acceptance criteria for related implementation tickets.
Include audit, permission, and state behavior in ticket definitions.
